name: Rebels RISE Scholar Program – Spring 2026 | Applications Now Open
description: Applications for the Spring 2026 Rebels RISE Scholar Cohort are now open. The Rebels RISE Scholar Program is a flexible, non-graded experience designed to help students strengthen their connection to campus, build community, and develop skills that support academic and personal success. Through online modules, optional in-person events, workshops, and individualized support, Rebels RISE encourages students to engage with campus resources and with one another during their time at UNLV. This program is open to UNLV students who have completed their first year at UNLV or have 30+ earned credits and are looking to deepen their campus involvement, build meaningful connections, and gain additional support as they continue their academic journey. Application Deadline: January 30, 2026 Apply today

name: EWB General Meeting
description: Come hang out with Engineers Without Borders UNLV for our first general meeting of the semester. We are kicking things off with food, good vibes, and big decisions. We will share quick updates on the projects we are officially wrapping up, then we will start choosing our next project, the one we plan to travel to. If you have ever wanted to be part of something meaningful (and actually get to see it in the real world), this is the meeting to come to. We are also excited to welcome our guest speaker Ed Miller, PE. Ed recently relocated to Las Vegas and has years of experience with Engineers Without Borders. He previously mentored the EWB student chapter at the University of Maryland, supported student design teams, served as an ICP, and participated in implementation trips in Ethiopia, Burkina Faso, Sierra Leone, and Peru. He will share what makes a strong new project and what to consider as we start our project selection process. What to expect -Free Food and Fun Vibes:)

name: "Working with Challenging Individuals"
description: This interactive workshop will help participants work more effectively with challenging people. Whether it is a passive-aggressive co-worker, an inept supervisor, a know-it-all colleague, or politically-minded schemer. We will discuss these and other common workplace personality types and, through small-group discussion, role plays, and collective problem-solving, learn how to be less frustrated and more functional, even when challenging people may abound.

name: Time Management #2 - Spring 26 Success Series
description: This workshop will help students build effective academic habits to support success throughout the semester. We will discuss motivation, goal-setting, and planning strategies to help avoid procrastination and manage time more effectively. Whether you’re a busy student juggling multiple responsibilities or simply looking to improve your productivity, this session is for you. The UNLV Writing Center will also join us to share strategies for building healthy writing habits, including making time for writing and staying consistent with assignments. Visit our website for additional information. https://www.unlv.edu/asc/coaching/workshops

name: Becoming an Adaptive Perfectionist
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Learn how to rethink the relationship between motivation and self-criticism, set manageable goals, and be more productive while also tending to your wellbeing.

name: Human Trafficking Prevention Club Meeting
description: The Human Trafficking Prevention Club at UNLV invites you to join us for our Spring 2026 Meetings. These meetings provide a safe and supportive space to learn about human trafficking, understand the warning signs, and explore how you can help prevent it within your community. Throughout the semester, we will engage in open discussions, educational activities, and guest speaker conversations focused on awareness, prevention, and advocacy. Whether you are new to this topic or already passionate about social justice, all are welcome! Our goal is to empower individuals with knowledge, encourage meaningful dialogue, and inspire action against human trafficking.

name: Human Trafficking Prevention Club Meeting
description: Join the Human Trafficking Prevention Club at UNLV for an informative and impactful meeting focused on understanding human trafficking in Southern Nevada. This meeting will feature guest speakers Jhenna Strasser, Manager of the Southern Nevada Human Trafficking Task Force, and Rachel Stephensen, LVMPD Senior Financial Analyst. Our speakers will share their professional experiences, explain how the Human Trafficking Task Force operates, and discuss real ways students can get involved in prevention, advocacy, and community action. This is a great opportunity for students interested in social justice, criminal justice, psychology, public service, or anyone who wants to learn to make a difference. We aim to create a safe and informed space where students can learn the signs of human trafficking and feel empowered to take action against it.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Opportunity Village
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Opportunity Village is a nonprofit organization that serves individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities in Southern Nevada. Through employment training, day programs, and community-based services, Opportunity Village empowers participants to build independence, develop job skills, and engage meaningfully with their community. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with hands-on projects that support Opportunity Village’s programs and daily operations. Tasks may include assembling materials, organizing workspaces, preparing supplies, or supporting light production or packaging activities. Volunteers may work individually or in small groups and will be assigned specific tasks by site staff. Important Information: This is an indoor service site. Volunteers should be comfortable standing for extended periods, performing repetitive tasks, and working respectfully alongside program participants and staff. All volunteers must follow site guidelines to ensure a safe, inclusive, and supportive environment. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Lake Mead
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Lake Mead National Recreation Area is one of the most visited national parks in the United States and plays a vital role in environmental conservation, water sustainability, and outdoor recreation in Southern Nevada. The site provides critical natural habitats, recreational access, and educational opportunities while facing ongoing environmental challenges such as litter, erosion, and water conservation concerns. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with outdoor environmental stewardship projects aimed at preserving the natural beauty and safety of Lake Mead. Tasks may include litter removal, trail or shoreline clean-up, and general site beautification efforts. Volunteers may work individually or in small groups and will be assigned specific areas or tasks by site staff. Important Information: This is an outdoor service site that may involve walking on uneven terrain, bending, standing, and light lifting. Volunteers should be comfortable working outdoors for the duration of the service experience and following all instructions provided by site staff. Weather conditions may vary, and volunteers should come prepared to stay hydrated and protected from the sun. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Project 150
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Project 150 is a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting homeless, displaced, and disadvantaged high school students in Southern Nevada. The organization provides free resources such as food, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and other essential support to help students stay in school and work toward graduation in a stable and dignified way. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with indoor organization and preparation projects that support Project 150’s daily operations. Tasks may include sorting and organizing clothing and supplies, assembling hygiene kits, stocking shelves, and preparing items for student distribution. Volunteers may work individually or in small groups and will be assigned specific tasks by site staff. Important Information: This is an indoor service site that involves standing, light lifting, and repetitive tasks. Volunteers should be comfortable working in a warehouse-style environment and following all instructions provided by Project 150 staff to ensure safety and efficiency. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Cure 4 The Kids
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Cure 4 The Kids is a nonprofit pediatric specialty clinic dedicated to providing comprehensive medical care to children with chronic and life-threatening illnesses. The clinic supports patients and families through compassionate, high-quality care and a child-centered environment. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with indoor cleaning and organization projects to support clinic operations. Tasks include deep cleaning exam rooms (including furniture and walls) and organizing the Child Life Specialist closet by sorting toys and supplies. Volunteers may be assigned individually or in small groups to specific rooms or tasks. Important Information: This is an indoor service site within an active medical clinic. Volunteers should be comfortable performing cleaning and organizing tasks and working respectfully in a healthcare environment. Light lifting, standing, and repetitive tasks may be required. All volunteers must follow staff instructions while on site. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Project Marilyn
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Project Marilyn is a nonprofit organization in Southern Nevada dedicated to supporting individuals experiencing homelessness by providing access to essential resources, advocacy, and community-based support. The organization focuses on restoring dignity and stability by addressing immediate needs while connecting individuals to longer-term support services. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with hands-on projects that support Project Marilyn’s outreach and operations. Tasks may include sorting and organizing donated items, assembling care kits, preparing supplies for distribution, and assisting with general facility organization. Volunteers may work individually or in small groups and will be assigned tasks by site staff. Important Information: This is an indoor service site that may involve standing, light lifting, and repetitive tasks. Volunteers should be comfortable working in a warehouse-style or storage environment and following all instructions provided by Project Marilyn staff to ensure safety and efficiency. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Three Square Food Bank
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Three Square Food Bank is Southern Nevada’s only food bank and a vital resource in the fight against hunger in our community. Through food distribution, community partnerships, and advocacy, Three Square works to ensure that individuals and families across the region have consistent access to nutritious food. Service Activities: Volunteers will support warehouse-based food distribution efforts by sorting, packing, and organizing food items. Tasks may include standing for extended periods and light lifting to help prepare food for distribution to community partners. Important Information: This service site is primarily indoors. Volunteers should be comfortable working in a warehouse environment and following food safety guidelines. All volunteers must wear long pants and closed-toe shoes. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Spring 2026 Service Day: Center for Urban Water Conservation
description: Date: Friday, February 6, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: The UNLV Center for Urban Water Conservation is an outdoor facility dedicated to promoting sustainable water use in the Las Vegas Valley through education, research, and community engagement. The center plays a key role in conservation efforts by supporting environmentally responsible practices and hands-on community learning. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with outdoor conservation work, including planting, seed harvesting, pruning, mulching, weeding, and general site cleanup. Activities may involve light lifting and working on uneven terrain. Important Information: This is a fully outdoor site with active honey bees present. Volunteers with known bee allergies must notify organizers in advance and carry an EpiPen if required. Long-sleeve shirts and pants are required. Sun protection and bringing enough water are strongly recommended. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Lunar New Year Mahjong Tournament
description: Lunar New Year Mahjong Tournament Join Chinese Corner in collaboration with the Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV for a Lunar New Year Mahjong Tournament celebrating strategy, culture, and community! This elimination-style tournament is open to UNLV students, faculty, staff, and community members with basic familiarity with Mahjong. Players will compete in structured rounds for a chance to place and win prizes. Awards for 1st through 4th place will be presented the same day, with the grand prize announced during the Lunar New Year Night Market. After tournament play concludes, the event will transition into open Mahjong free play, welcoming spectators and casual players who want to learn, observe, or enjoy the game in a relaxed setting. Light refreshments will be provided. Whether you’re a seasoned Mahjong player or simply interested in cultural games and Lunar New Year traditions, this event offers an engaging and social experience. 🎴 Tournament Play: 10:15 AM – approximately 2:15 PM 🎴 Open Play: 2:00 PM – 4:00 PM 🎴 Registration is limited to 32 players

name: Burrowing Owl Den Clean Up!
description: Join UNLVolunteers and the Birding and Conservation Club at the Rainbow Owl Preserve to help clean out the nest chambers of artificial owl burrows for the start of the 2026 breeding season. For birders, this is a great opportunity for owl viewing and identification (and for everyone else, the owls are pretty cute). This will also be a great opportunity for owl viewing and identification of owl signs (pellets, bones, white wash, feathers, etc.) The work includes using shovels to remove dirt above the nest chamber, removing debris or plants blocking the chamber, and getting dirty. Shovels, headlamps, and tools will be provided but participants should bring gloves, water, appropriate clothing for getting dirty and remember to wear sunscreen. Transportation is provided up to ten people, please email birding@unlv.edu or erica.mann@unlv.edu for carpool information. Food/snacks will also be provided. The location of this event is Rainbow Owl Preserve, the location does not show up on maps so we will meet on the corner of Donald Nelson & Rio Vista. This event will run from 9 AM to 1 PM, with carpooling meeting at 8 @ the UNLV Student Union. This event is open to all UNLV students, spots are limited so RSVP on the involvement center website by 2/6!

This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "Should companies be held liable for job displacement caused by AI implementation?" and "Should one’s social media impact their employment?"

name: General Meeting - Club Revival

Whether you are a freshman looking for a community or a senior wanting to leave a legacy, this meeting is your chance to help build the foundation of our chapter. What to Expect: • The Vision: We’ll outline our roadmap for the year, including professional development workshops, networking events, and community outreach. • Leadership Opportunities: We are looking for passionate members to step into foundational roles and help lead the relaunch. • Networking: Connect with fellow engineers and STEM majors who share your background and drive. • Resources: Learn about SHPE National benefit

name: Off Campus Bird Walk: Finding Lovebirds
description: Join UNLV BCC for a relaxing bird walk at the Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve! As spring arrives in Las Vegas, many birds are busy trying to find their true love for the spring season. This is the best time to observe birds out in the open and see some unique behavior! We will be spotting some of the "lovebirds" that have decided to set up their families at the Preserve while learning about different rituals and displays birds do to attract a mate. Like always, binoculars will be provided as supplies last, in addition the Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve also has binoculars to borrow! We will be walking outside for this event so please be sure to bring water, sun protection, a hat, and comfortable shoes! Due to the off-campus nature of the event, we will require waivers to be filled out before the event date. Carpooling will be available for this event! If you want the opportunity to carpool to the event location please RSVP by February 11th!

Through Coffee with Karly, you will: ✅ Build authentic connections through casual conversation and community-building. ✅ Reflect on your personal leadership journey and how everyday choices can create change. ✅ Strengthen your understanding of how relationships and mentorship shape leadership development. ✅ Explore the role of dialogue and connection in fostering inclusive, community-centered leadership. ✅ Identify one way to nurture your leadership skills through intentional relationships and mentorship. Whether you come every month or drop in when you can, we’re here to sip, chat, and grow—together.

name: LeaderShape: Resilience Workshop
description: Just like Alex, Marty, Gloria, and Melman had to adapt when they found themselves far from the comforts of the Central Park Zoo, Resilience is all about learning how to navigate the wild twists and turns of life. In this program, participants will explore the LeaderShape Model of Resilience to discover their core values—the anchors that keep us steady when things feel unpredictable. Together, we’ll identify the challenges that call for resilience, learn how to rewrite our own stories with confidence (kind of like Julien declaring himself king of the lemurs), and reflect on the ways we can build strength both within ourselves and alongside our communities. By the end, you’ll not only recognize your own resilience but also leave ready to co-create it with your team—because in the jungle of change, we’re better when we move it, move it together.

name: It's Just Coffee: Mental Health in America
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "What is the best way to address mental health in the United States?"

name: Black Mini Con 🌈🖤✨
description: Black Mini Con is a mini conference featuring interactive sessions centering Black LGBTQIA+ identity, Black mental health, resilience, and belonging. This event creates space for learning, healing, and affirmation through shared experience. Session 1 (11 AM-12:15 PM) Title: Queer As Black – Black As Queer Facilitated by: Christopher J Edwards Description: Queer As Black. Black As Queer is a facilitated conversation centering the lived experiences of Black LGBTQIA+ people and affirming that Blackness is not a monolith. This session explores how Black queer identities have been historically erased and misunderstood, even within Black spaces, while also honoring the resilience, joy, and complexity of the community. Through guided dialogue and reflection, participants are invited into honest conversation about belonging, accountability, and liberation. This space is rooted in collective understanding, healing, and connection. Lunch 12:15-12:45PM Session 2 (12:45 PM-2 PM) Title: Thriving while Black: Mental Health, Identity, and Resilience Facilitated by: Bianca Broomfield and Dr. Shawn Mines Description: This interactive presentation centers Black mental health within the context of campus life, with a focus on navigating predominantly white spaces, colorism, and ethnic identity development. Through guided reflection, discussion, and shared dialogue, participants will explore how these experiences shape well-being, belonging, and self-concept for Black students. Attendees will leave with practical tools, affirming language, and community-informed strategies to protect their mental health and build resilience. The session creates space for connection, validation, and collective learning. 🍽️ Lunch provided by Calabash African Kitchen 📅 February 24 ⏰ 11:00 AM–2:00 PM 📍 Barrick Museum Auditorium ✊🏾 RSVP Today

name: UNLV Career & Internship Fair Spring 2026
description: UNLV students and alumni are invited to attend the Career & Internship Fair, where you'll meet employers from a wide range of industries who are hiring for full-time, part-time, and internship positions. Before the Fair: How to Prepare Pre-register on Handshake to receive important updates and reminders. Research employers attending the fair—come prepared with a question or two. Update your resume and have it reviewed by Career Services. Update your Handshake profile and upload your resume. Practice your elevator pitch so you're ready to introduce yourself with confidence. Meet with a career coach by booking through Handshake to ensure you're fully prepared. Tips for the Day of the Fair Bring your smartphone to check in at booths and share your resume via the Handshake app. Bring printed copies of your resume for employers who don’t use Handshake. Dress professionally and make a strong first impression. Be prepared to introduce yourself—be friendly, confident, and brief. Be mindful of time—keep conversations focused and let others have a turn. Connect with employers. Explore opportunities. Take the next step in your career. Register here on Handshake: https://unlv.joinhandshake.com/edu/career_fairs/62101

name: Spring Student Union Showcase
description: Student Union & Event Services is excited to host our first Spring Student Union Showcase, highlighting our event spaces in the Student Union and across campus, the services we can offer clients to complete their vision, and catering samples through UNLV Eats. The showcase has been designed with your event's success and schedule in mind offering the same workshops and panels in both the morning and afternoon session blocks to help educate you on best practices for hosting meetings and events. Registered Student Organizations, Departments, and Non-UNLV clients are welcome to register online at: https://web.cvent.com/event/a9a9a1ef-fde8-4eb2-a36a-cd3a02c01f7b/register Below is a brief description of what each session will be about. We look forward to seeing you there! Please note: these sessions will not be recorded. Session Block 1 & 4 What do we wish people knew when they call us? Panel (8:40am-9:10am, 2:10pm-2:40pm) We receive emails and phone calls all the time for very similar questions our clients have. While we of course love to help our clients, this panel will address these common questions our clients have that we wish they knew before they called us. Conference & Intern Housing FAQ (8:40am-9:10am, 2:10pm-2:40pm) The summer time is the best time to host overnight stays in on-campus housing for your conference or camp. This FAQ session will answer your questions pertaining to conference and intern housing for this and future summer stays. Tales of Event Success Virtual Panel (8:40am-9:10am, 2:10pm-2:40pm) Featuring RSO, department, and non-UNLV event organizers, this panel will discuss tips and tricks our clients have picked up over the years hosting their events with us. Session Block 2 & 5 UNLV Eats Catering (9:20am-9:50am, 2:50pm-3:20pm) Learn about the catering options available for reservations and events of all sizes and budgets. Marketing in the SU (9:20am-9:50am, 2:50pm-3:20pm) Receive information on and be able to ask questions about the marketing options we can provide UNLV and non-UNLV clients alike in and around the Student Union. Free Speech Virtual Panel (9:20am-9:50am, 2:50pm-3:20pm) This panel, aimed at both UNLV and non-UNLV clients, will discuss hosting events on campus that pertain to your first amendment rights. Session Block 3 & 6 Event Planning 101 (10am-10:30am, 3:30pm-4pm) A crash course in event planning from initial idea through day-of the event. Creating Inclusive Events (10am-10:30am, 3:30pm-4pm) When designing your event, are you thinking of guests who may need special accommodations or dining options that satisfy all appetites? This panel, led by universal design, will discuss best approaches to make your event welcoming for all attendees. RMS/UPD Best Practices (10am-10:30am, 3:30pm-4pm) Risk management is an integral part of the event planning process. This FAQ panel featuring risk management, insurance, and university police experts will address concerns related to the safety of your guests and event. If you may have any questions, please contact via email at taylor.cayro@unlv.edu or 702-895-4449.

description: Join MIS Society on March 4, 2026, from 5:45–7:00 PM for a guest speaker session on cybersecurity and IT security policy featuring Michael, a cybersecurity and policy professional with extensive experience in emergency management and homeland security. Michael holds a B.S. in Political Science from Towson University and an MSL in Cybersecurity Law and Policy from the University of Maryland Francis King Carey School of Law. He currently serves as a Security Manager with SAFE Management Nevada at Allegiant Stadium and previously worked as a Senior Policy Analyst at the University of Maryland Center for Health and Homeland Security. From 2022–2026, he was a voting member of the Maryland State 9-1-1 Board and Chair of its Cybersecurity Subcommittee, and he also teaches as a Cybersecurity Adjunct Instructor with the Texas A&M Engineering Extension Service. Free pizza will be provided, and this event qualifies for IS professional participation credit.

name: It's Just Coffee: Legalizing Sex Work

This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "Should sex work be legalized in Nevada or should it be limited to current rural counties?"

name: Guest Speaker: Dr. Lina Nih's Research on the Future of Stroke Recovery and Neural Repair
description: We are thrilled to announce an open Q&A session with guest speaker Dr. Lina Nih, PhD! Affiliated with the UNLV Department of Brain Health and the Kirk Kerkorian School of Medicine, Dr. Nih is a neuroscientist and vascular biologist with a focus on biomedical engineering and materials science. She currently leads the N.I.H. (Neuro-regenerative Innovative Health) Laboratory, and she has been previously affiliated with UCLA, the David School of Medicine-Neurology Department, and the Cellular and Biomolecular Engineering Department. Join us for a lecture and Q&A session with Dr. Nih on brain healing, innovation, and finding your place in research! This event is open to the public, and we encourage you to invite your friends, classmates, lab-mates, and anyone else interested in groundbreaking neuroscience research! Spots will be limited, so fill the form out ASAP to e nsure your spot!

name: "Interpersonal Communication: Say What You Mean"
description: In this interactive workshop, participants explore three critical aspects of effective communication. First, we focus on bridging the gap between the intended message, the actual delivery, and the recipient's perception by providing valuable tips to ensure clear and successful communication. Then we explore how nonverbal cues, like tone, body language, and timing, can enhance or undermine a message. Finally, we explore the pros and cons of email communication, uncovering potential pitfalls and benefits. Each segment includes group discussions and role plays to help participants incorporate the concepts into daily practice.

This session will be hosted by Aparna Polavarapu, professor of law and co-director of the Saltman Center of Conflict Resolution at William S. Boyd School of Law. Reflective Letter Writing Workshop: Following the discussion, the Care Center will transition into a creative workshop. Participants will be invited to write a letter to their past or future selves, or to someone else, as a way to externalize feelings and set intentions for the future. Help create a collaborative artwork that will be exhibited at the Barrick Museum! Date: Wednesday, March 25 | 1–3:30 PM Location: Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Cost: Free and open to the public Follow us on Instagram for more updates: @carecenter_snv

name: Caribbean Students Association~ Sounds of the Caribbean: Leadership & Activism through Conscious Reggae
description: Join us for Sounds of the Caribbean, a celebration of artists as community leaders and the powerful role of conscious reggae music in shaping political awareness, social change, and community identity across the Black diaspora. This program highlights Bob Marley, whose music and message of unity, resistance, and empowerment continue to inspire communities worldwide. Through his songs and those of other conscious reggae artists, participants will explore how music serves as a tool for leadership—uplifting voices, challenging injustice, and guiding communities toward collective action. The event also spotlights unsung leaders of the Caribbean diaspora, musicians and cultural storytellers whose contributions may not always be widely recognized but have profoundly influenced their communities. From calypso to soca and reggae, Caribbean musical traditions demonstrate that leadership extends beyond formal roles—it emerges through creativity, cultural expression, and the power of shared stories. Come discover how rhythm, storytelling, and conscious music create movements, build communities, and inspire change.

name: Stand Out in the Room: Own Your Professionalism

In this interactive virtual workshop, you’ll learn how to communicate with clarity, confidence, and authenticity- without freezing, rambling, or sounding scripted. From first impressions to behavioral questions to “Tell me about yourself,” we’ll break down how to share your story in a way that feels natural and professional. Plus, get tips for networking messages and follow-up emails that actually sound like you. Perfect for job seekers, internship hunters, and anyone ready to own their story out loud. Join us in the SRWC Room 1020 from 6 pm to 8 pm to make your very first stinky perfume in honor of April Fools' Day! We will be making scents with a funny twist, so come ready with your sleeves rolled and your mind open for craziness! *Don't forget to RSVP* Please join the GroupMe through the front page link or the QR code on the event flyer to stay up-to-date with club updates and get further location information.

name: Leadership Link Up ~ Leading Through Change - Shinobi Style
description: Just like Naruto grew from an underdog academy student to a respected Hokage, true leaders learn to adapt, persevere, and guide their squads through every mission—no matter how unpredictable the path. Whether you’re navigating personal growth arcs or helping your team move through major transitions, this session will help you tap into your inner ninja and lead with resilience, empathy, and clarity. Participants will: Recognize the “Shinobi Cycle of Change” — understanding how emotional phases (from confusion to clarity) affect individuals and entire squads. Reflect on personal adaptability by identifying your own strengths, blind spots, and “chakra drains” when facing transformation. Develop leadership strategies inspired by shinobi teamwork—learning how to support others with empathy, communicate clearly, and guide your team through uncertain missions. ​

name: Donut Worry, We Care! Care Center Open House
location: Student Services Complex Building A | Room: 255
description: The Care Center warmly invites all students, faculty, and staff to our open house! S top by to grab free donuts, tour our facilities, meet our team, and learn about the wide range of services we offer to support students, faculty, and staff impacted by domestic violence, relationship violence, sexual violence, or stalking. All attendees will also be entered into an opportunity drawing for an exciting giveaway! This open house is the perfect chance to ask questions, discover ways to get involved during Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and connect with the Care Center team, whether you’re curious about our resources or want to learn about ways to support your community.

name: VIRTUAL Success Series: Bouncing back from Burnout
description: College can get overwhelming, and burnout is real. In Bouncing Back from Burnout, students will learn how to recognize burnout, manage stress in healthier ways, and build habits that support both their personal well-being and academic success. This workshop will provide practical tools, self-care tips, and helpful campus resources to help students feel more balanced and supported.

name: Human Trafficking Prevention Club Meeting
description: The Human Trafficking Prevention Club at UNLV invites you to join us for our Spring 2026 Meetings. These meetings provide a safe and supportive space to learn about human trafficking, understand the warning signs, and explore how you can help prevent it within your community. Throughout the semester, we will engage in open discussions, educational activities, and guest speaker conversations focused on awareness, prevention, and advocacy. Whether you are new to this topic or already passionate about social justice, all are welcome! Our goal is to empower individuals with knowledge, encourage meaningful dialogue, and inspire action against human trafficking.

We’ll provide all the ingredients and guidance, you just bring your appetite and a willingness to try something new. Come connect with others, share some laughs, and enjoy the tasty rewards of your creations together! Registration is required and the form can be found in our Discord.

As a student org-sponsored event, we will require that those entering the pool area fill out the standard UNLV liability waiver.

organization: Student Support Club for Academic Success
description: Room ASC 146, All free to join!

name: Field Trip: St. Thomas, NV
description: Join the Anthropology Society for a fun and educational walk about St. Thomas, a once-thriving community before Lake Mead was created! Enjoy a guided walk through the ruins of the town with Park Rangers who know a thing or two about the history of this unique feature in the Nevada desert. Be prepared to spend hours outdoors - bring plenty of water, good hiking shoes, sunscreen, hats, and a lunch. Rides can be provided by the UNLV Anthropology Department if we have a headcount by March 20, 2026. Cars will leave UNLV no later than 8:15 am, so please be on time!

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

description: Please come and join UNLVolunteers to sort and organize comic books with Critical Care Comics. 12:00 PM – 12:15 PM: Educational session — learn about the organization, comic sorting process, and how your efforts help the community. 12:15 PM – 12:20 PM: Wrap-up and prepare for departure. 12:20 PM – 12:30 PM: Leave for the site. 1:00 PM: Arrive at the comic sorting site. 1:00 PM – 3:00 PM: Sorting comics. 3:30 PM: Arrive back at the UNLV Student Union.

name: Conference Proposal Writing Workshop
description: Grad Ready Series: Elevate Your Research: Master the Conference Proposal From Abstract to Podium. Conference proposal submission season is coming up. Navigating the transition from research to presentation is a critical milestone in your academic career. BGSA and the Writing Center invite you to a specialized session focused on the nuances of writing winning conference proposals for graduate-level symposiums and national conferences. Why Attend? The Reviewer’s Perspective: Understand what selection committees look for in a competitive abstract. Strategic Framing: Learn how to articulate your methodology and significance within strict word counts. Disciplinary Clarity: Tailor your pitch to suit both niche and interdisciplinary audiences. 📍 Session Details Date: April 3rd, 2026 Time: 4:00pm - 5:00pm Location: Online Target Audience: Current Masters and Doctoral candidates across all disciplines. Pro Tip: Bring a current abstract or a project description you are working on, so that you can ask specific questions.

This exciting event celebrates creativity, skill, and personal expression through a variety of engaging presentations and performances. Open to all UNLV students, families, and the wider community, the event is FREE, the showcase highlights the unique talents and passions of our OTD students as they share projects, demonstrations, and activities that reflect the meaningful ways they engage in life and work. Attendees can expect an evening filled with energy, inspiration, and a sense of connection, as participants explore the diverse ways occupational therapy enhances everyday life. Whether you’re a student, family member, or community supporter, this event is a wonderful opportunity to experience the creativity, innovation, and dedication of our future occupational therapists in a lively and welcoming environment. Come celebrate the meaningful occupations that shape our lives! Please click this link to receive your free tickets: Showcase of Meaningful Occupations

name: It's Just Coffee: Athletics at University
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "How much should coaches get paid?" and "Should it be the university's responsibility to equitably fund sports?"

name: Xiao Jiao Corner
description: 🐼 Xiao Jiao Corner – Practice Chinese Together 一起练习中文 | All Levels Welcome Looking for a relaxed and supportive space to practice Chinese? Join us at Xiao Jiao Corner, a welcoming environment where students of all proficiency levels can grow their language skills together. Whether you're just starting out or already conversational, this is a low-pressure space to build confidence and improve through practice. 🌟 What We’ll Do: Practice speaking Chinese in small groups Practice writing Chinese characters Receive peer feedback Ask questions before turning in assignments Connect with fellow Chinese language learners 💛 Why It Works: No pressure No judgment Learn at your own pace Supportive, student-led environment Come for one session or join us regularly — we’re here to help each other succeed!

Read or bring a research paper (physics-related), and note down things you found interesting or exciting. After a set amount of time, you'll move on to another partner to discuss your papers with!

name: Breathe in with Obodo
description: Join UNLVolunteers on April 10th from 9 AM to 1 PM for an afternoon of gardening with Obodo Collective. Take a breath before the stress of the last stretch of the semester. Spend the morning gardening and wind down with a grounding exercise and refreshments. The following items are suggested to ensure a fun, safe, and comfortable experience: Close toe shoes Head covering and sunglasses A light long sleeve shirt or arm sleeves Light, durable pants, loose fit pants

name: Serve & Lead Mini-Con 2026: Taking Flight
description: The Serve & Lead MiniCon is a one-day conference designed to help UNLV students connect their community engagement and leadership development experiences with the careers and callings that await them. 2026 MiniCon - Taking Flight This year’s theme - Taking Flight - symbolizes how small actions, intentional design, and supportive winds can lift students into their next chapter with confidence and direction. It is also fitting for our first attempt to get the Mini-Con off the ground. Just as a paper airplane is crafted, adjusted, and launched with purpose, the Mini-Con empowers students to shape their own trajectory. Through interactive workshops, panel discussions, and hands-on engagement opportunities, participants will explore how service, community involvement, and leadership roles become the folds and angles that give structure to their professional identities. Attendees will learn to articulate the transferable skills gained through service work and leadership activities, and understand how these experiences create lift in any workplace or avocation. Whether students are still experimenting with the best design, catching an updraft, or ready for takeoff, the Serve & Lead Mini-Con offers the tools, networks, and inspiration to support their ascent. Join us as we explore how serving others builds stronger leaders—and how your unique story can take flight into a future driven by purpose, passion, and impact. NOTE: UNLV students are encouraged to attend for as much of the event as possible, but are not required to stay the entire time as we understand many will have class, work, or other obligations. Please RSVP even if you are only able to attend a session or two - we will be happy to have you! Full Event Details and Session Descriptions Event Agenda at a Glance: Time Activity 9:00 - 9:20 am Check-in opens, breakfast, sign-up for service projects 9:20 - 9:30 am Welcome and Introducing Keynote 9:30 - 9:50 am Keynote Speaker 9:50 - 10 am Break/Transition Time 10 am - 11 am Service Project Breakouts 11 - 12 pm Lunch & Guest Panel 12 - 12:45 pm Leadership Breakouts 12:45 - 12:55 pm Break/Transition Time 12:55 - 1:40 pm Leadership Breakouts 1:40 - 1:45 pm Break/Transition Time 1:45 - 2 pm Opportunity Drawing and Closing

name: The Wildlife Society-Seed Collection at Lake Mead National Recreation Area
description: Join National Park Service biologists and nursery staff to assist with collecting native seeds that will be used for future restoration and vegetation projects. These efforts directly support habitat restoration, and collected seeds can last for years when properly stored. This is a great opportunity to: Gain hands-on field experience in restoration ecology Work alongside National Park Service professionals Learn about native plant species and seed collection methods Contribute to real conservation efforts in southern Nevada Event Details Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026 Location: Lake Mead National Recreation Area Time & exact meeting location: Will be provided by the National Park Service one week prior to the event Please note that TWS officers will not be present at this event, but we encourage anyone interested to attend independently. We will also be hosting another field event on April 25, where TWS officers will be present and coordinating participation.

name: 2026 Intercollegiate Big Day Competition!
description: Join UNLV Birding, NSU Flight Club and CSN Birding Club to participate in the Spring Intercollegiate Bird Day Competition! This is a birding competition between different universities within the US! As the only Nevada representative we will be going around bird hot spots to see how many bird species we can discover. We will be traveling to the Clark County Wetlands Park early morning to catch all the migratory birds near a riparian environment then heading to the Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve to catch some exposed water fowl. Come learn about the species of Las Vegas and improve your birding skills with us! Binoculars are available to be borrowed! Before the event, please create an account on eBird and download the phone application. This will be the main way we keep track of attendance and bird species identified! Sign up and download here: https://ebird.org/home Take a peak at the trip report on the event! - https://ebird.org/tripreport/472119 Here are two more applications that can help you ID, be sure to download them before the event! iNaturalist: https://www.inaturalist.org/ Merlin Bird ID: https://merlin.allaboutbirds.org/ Please RSVP to this event by April 8th! We will be sending out waivers before the event that you will need to sign, and an information request about your eBird profile to add you to the report, so we can get straight into birding!

You'll gain tools to reframe challenges, embrace curiosity, and take small, intentional steps towards a life that truly resonates with you, no matter what major you're pursuing. name: The Brain Health Research Pipeline Panel
description: We are so excited to announce our first ever Brain Health Research Pipeline Panel! Meet and speak with graduate students, laboratory technicians, post-doctoral researchers, and other faculty including Dr. Kinney himself from the Kinney Lab (otherwise known as the Pam Quirk Biomarker Discovery Laboratory). Holding numerous positions on campus, Dr. Jefferson Kinney serves as NURO's club advisor, founding chair of the Department of Brain Health, and co-director of the Pam Quirk Brain Health and Biomarker Laboratory! This event will begin with an informative career-oriented presentation on the neuroscience research pipeline followed by an open Q&A session, so bring your questions and your notebooks–any and all curiosity is welcomed and encouraged! This event will also be open to the public, so feel free to bring along friends, peers, and anyone that you think might be interested! Spots will be limited, so please RSVP with the link below. Participants rotate through five fast-paced rounds—developing a business name, product or service, target audience, cultural element, and monetization strategy—while building on ideas created by other teams. At the end of the event, teams receive their original concept back, now evolved through collaboration, and deliver a final pitch. This event encourages creativity, adaptability, and entrepreneurial thinking while incorporating cross-cultural perspectives in business development.

name: From Inner Mongolia to Las Vegas: Searching for Human Universals Across Dueling Cultures
description: Drawing on decades of fieldwork and lived experiences, this informal talk reflects on what Dr. Jankowiak has learned studying cultural life in very different social worlds: north China, Mormon polygamous families, Las Vegas swingers, and mainstream American society. Moving between these settings raises a persistent anthropological question: how different are people across cultures, and where do common human patterns emerge? By revisiting these encounters, Dr. Jankowiak explores what comparative experience reveals about culture, intimacy, and the possibility of human universals.

There are limited spots, so RSVP today and bring a friend or two! As the date approaches, we will follow up and provide additional information. Questions? Email altbreaks@unlv.edu

name: Taichi and Football Seminar
description: Join the Sands Institute for Chinese Language and Culture at UNLV, in collaboration with Chinese Corner, for a unique seminar exploring the connection between traditional Tai Chi and modern athletic performance. Participants will gain insight into improving balance, coordination, flexibility, and mental focus while learning techniques that support injury prevention, recovery, and overall physical resilience. Through concepts such as force absorption, redirection, and optimized body mechanics, attendees will discover how Tai Chi can be applied both on and off the field to elevate performance and well-being.

description: DASH is one of the longest-running, key signature programs here at UNLV. Join UNLVolunteers as we build peanut butter & jelly sandwiches lunch bags to hand out during the community lunch hour at the Salvation Army! We will be meeting at the Student Union 1st floor, near the eating bar at 11:15am to begin our meal prep shift. Volunteers will participate in a short educational activity followed immediately by preparing lunch bags to hand out at the Salvation Army. After the program, we hope participants will take their involvement to the next level by seeking other ways to do service and seeking to provide long-term solutions to social inequalities such as food and housing insecurity. PLEASE NOTE: Meal Prep shift will only prepare the sandwiches and sack lunches. In order to deliver, YOU MUST sign up for the delivery shift for this day's DASH event. ALL volunteers are expected to follow health standards and expectations ( hair net, beard guard and gloves).

name: SPS Semester Wrap Potluck and Movie
description: Join us for a fun semester wrap up with food and entertainment! We'll be watching Hidden Figures in BPB 217 and enjoying food! Feel free to bring snacks and drinks if you can!

name: DASH - Delivery Shift ONLY
description: DASH is one of the longest-running, key signature programs here at UNLV. After the program, we hope participants will take their involvement to the next level by seeking other ways to do service and seeking to provide long-term solutions to social inequalities such as food and housing insecurity. The Delivery shift begins at 12:45pm. Please plan on being here on time as we shortly take off to arrive at the Salvation Army by 1:15pm. Volunteers at the Salvation Army will volunteer to help out serving and handing out food and DASH sack lunches, as well as covering whatever other volunteer needs Salvation Army may need. Please plan on wearing long-sleeve shirt and pants at the Salvation Army. No exceptions to the pants rule allowed. Volunteers will receive 2.5 hours of service for participating.

Date: April 24 (Friday) Time: 7:00 PM – 8:30 PM Location: Student Union 211 Whether you’re a trivia expert or just coming for the fun, everyone is welcome! Light refreshments will be provided. We hope to see you there for a great night of laughter and teamwork.

name: The Wildlife Society-Seed Collection at Lake Mead National Recreation Area April 25
description: Join National Park Service biologists and nursery staff to assist with collecting native seeds that will be used for future restoration and vegetation projects. These efforts directly support habitat restoration, and collected seeds can last for years when properly stored. This is a great opportunity to: Gain hands-on field experience in restoration ecology Work alongside National Park Service professionals Learn about native plant species and seed collection methods Contribute to real conservation efforts in southern Nevada Event Details Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026 Location: Lake Mead National Recreation Area Time & exact meeting location: Will be provided by the National Park Service one week prior to the event

name: Wetlands Park 4th Annual BioBlast
description: Come join the Clark County Wetlands Park in their 4th Annual BioBast event! BioBlast is a free, all-ages, whole-family event that encourages the discovery of birds and more! Celebrate World Migratory Bird Day and learn about the plants and animals of Wetlands Park. Visit Science Stations, where guest scientists and naturalists will help you find and identify fish, bugs, plants, birds, and mammals. The Nature Center will feature lots of games and activities, too! UNLV BCC will be hosting a table at the event! Come out and show your support!

name: Student Dialogue & Brunch
description: Join us for an intentional gathering designed to foster meaningful conversations, genuine connections, and community support. Student Dialogue & Brunch brings together members of the African diaspora in Las Vegas for an open discussion on mental health challenges and practical coping strategies among students. In a relaxed and welcoming environment, attendees will engage with peers, connect with professionals, and build relationships that extend beyond the event. Whether you’re seeking support, community, or simply a space to be heard, this event creates an opportunity to connect, reflect, and grow together.

name: UNLV Women Veteran Retreat hosted by the Rebel Vets - April 25, 2026
description: 🌟 UNLV Women Veteran Retreat 🌟 Resiliency • Enlighten • Empower Calling All Women Veterans! The UNLV Rebel Vets invite women veterans from campus and the community to attend a special Women Veteran Retreat to celebrate the strength, accomplishments, and individuality of those who have served. This unique gathering will be held at the UNLV Tam Alumni Center, creating a welcoming space for female veteran students and alumni women veterans to connect, recharge, and build lasting relationships. This retreat is more than an event—it’s a chance to step away from the daily routine, strengthen resiliency, and empower one another through shared experiences. 💬 What to Expect ✨ Three inspiring women veteran guest speakers representing: Social Work Entrepreneurship Vet Center employment pathways 🎉 Games, raffles, and interactive activities 🎶 Music and social time 🥗 Refreshments provided 🤝 Networking and connection with fellow women veterans Participants will gain new perspectives, learn about career pathways, and share meaningful conversations with women who understand the journey of military service and transition. 🌺 A Space Designed for Women Veterans This event is a women veterans-only gathering to foster open dialogue, support, and authentic connection. 📅 Join Us Come ready for a day of learning, laughter, reflection, and community as we celebrate the powerful network of women veterans at UNLV and throughout Southern Nevada. Resiliency. Enlighten. Empower. 📲 RSVP below Hosted by UNLV Rebel Vets. See you there!

name: Spring Semester Bird Walk!
description: Join UNLV BCC on our monthly on-campus bird walks! We will learn about the birds and diversity right here on UNLV's main campus. This event is great for beginners and experienced birders and will be a great introduction to identifying species. Anyone is free to join! The walks start at 9 AM and we will meet at the Flashlight Statue near HAM hall. Binoculars are provided! Invite your friends, and we'll see you there!

name: Introduction to AI Agents Using LangChain
description: AI & Data Science Club – Next Meeting Join us for our next AI & Data Science Club meeting, where we’ll explore an introduction to AI agents using LangChain. This beginner-friendly session will walk through core concepts and practical applications, making it a great opportunity to learn and get hands-on experience with modern AI tools. Bring your laptop, come ready to learn, and enjoy bagels while connecting with others interested in AI and data science.

name: SORCE Workshop: Officer Transitions
description: Don't leave the next crew hanging! Join us for a chill transition sesh to download your wisdom and set the new officers up for a win. Monday, April 27th from 11:30am-12:30p in the SORCE Room (SU 305). You can also join by Zoom: https://unlv.zoom.us/j/93958894803

name: Global Grads & Friends: End-of-Semester Coffee Meetup ☕🌍
description: Join us for a special end-of-semester event as we bring together graduate and international students to celebrate the close of another successful term! Taking place on April 27 at 2:00 PM at the Student Union Starbucks, this event is designed to create a warm and welcoming space for connection and community. Come together with fellow students to reflect on your experiences, share stories, and enjoy meaningful conversations in a relaxed setting. This event is a chance to celebrate your achievements, build new connections, and strengthen the sense of belonging within our global student community. Whether you stop by for a quick chat or stay longer to connect, we look forward to celebrating with you!

name: VIRTUAL Success Series: Finishing Strong
description: Finish the semester with confidence! In this workshop, you’ll learn practical strategies to prepare for the final weeks, effectively review course material, manage your time, and reduce stress. Event Vision We’ll also share tips on staying motivated, prioritizing tasks, and maintaining balance during one of the busiest times of the semester. Plus, the Writing Center will be joining us to share their expertise, along with helpful tools and tricks for strengthening your writing and successfully completing final papers and assignments. Set yourself up for success, feel more in control of your workload, and finish the semester on a strong and confident note. Students will receive a Google Calendar invite to join the session. Please remember to accept the invite so it is saved to your calendar. We look forward to seeing you there!

name: Fellowship & Connection: Friendship, Family, and Society
description: A guided presentation and discussion examining the relationship, conflict, and overlap between friendship, family, and society. Participants will be invited to share experiences and perspectives in an open dialogue setting. Open to everyone. 📅 April 27 | 1:30 PM–3:00 PM 📍 SU 228 RSVP Today!

name: Yoga Support Group (Care Advocates Recruitment)

The CARE Advocate RSO hopes you'll attend to meet fellow students, destress, come together in community, learn a thing a thing or two about yoga, and inquire about becoming an advocate or general member! If you have any questions or concerns regarding this event, please feel free to contact us at: carecenter@unlv.edu

name: Xiao Jiao Corner
description: 🐼 Xiao Jiao Corner – Practice Chinese Together 一起练习中文 | All Levels Welcome Looking for a relaxed and supportive space to practice Chinese? Join us at Xiao Jiao Corner, a welcoming environment where students of all proficiency levels can grow their language skills together. Whether you're just starting out or already conversational, this is a low-pressure space to build confidence and improve through practice. 🌟 What We’ll Do: Practice speaking Chinese in small groups Practice writing Chinese characters Receive peer feedback Ask questions before turning in assignments Connect with fellow Chinese language learners 💛 Why It Works: No pressure No judgment Learn at your own pace Supportive, student-led environment Come for one session or join us regularly — we’re here to help each other succeed!

name: Business Law Panel
description: Join us for an engaging afternoon dedicated to the world of business law. We are excited to host a panel of esteemed professionals who will share their unique career journeys, the lessons they’ve learned in the field, and their best advice for those looking to follow a similar path. This is more than just a presentation—it is your chance to hear real-world stories, ask questions during our Q&A session, and gain valuable insights into the legal industry. Our Featured Panelists: Lori Johnson (Professor of Law at UNLV Boyd School of Law) Erin Barnet (Vice President, Corporate Counsel at Fontainebleau Las Vegas) Austin K. F. Benner (Associate at Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani) Tyler Stevens (Associate at Greenberg Traurig) Kirche Ray (Associate at Greenberg Traurig). 📅 Date: April 27th 🕒 Time: 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM 📍 Location: HOS 384 This event is open to ALL students interested in law, business, or litigation (Membership is not required to attend). Questions? Email us at blawassociation@gmail.com. Stay Connected: 📱 IG: @Unlvbla

name: The Exchange: Where Opportunities Begin
description: A student-led event hosted by International Business Association and AI in Business, and sponsored by RebelBot. The Exchange brings together students, faculty, and professionals for meaningful conversations, new connections, and real opportunities. The goal is to create a space where people can connect across industries, share resources, explore internships and career paths, and build relationships that actually lead somewhere. What to expect: Networking and community building Guest speakers Open mic for opportunities, resources, and announcements A look at RebelBot Date: Monday, April 27 Time: 5:30 PM – 8:10 PM Location: Lee Business School, UNLV RSVP: https://luma.com/r0a2l5sd Open Mic Interest Form: https://forms.gle/bFBQH93iZkCXemjG6 Try RebelBot: https://rebelbot.ai

name: It's Just Coffee: Partisanship in American Politics
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "What do we have to give up to get the political system we want?" and "Should we reform politics and government to encourage compromise, or will that mean giving up on the changes we really need and want?"

name: Rainbow Story Hour
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Remember the good ole’ days of being a kid and heading to your favorite library for storytime? You're in luck! Join us for our first Rainbow Story Hour – storytime for LGBTQ+ students and Allies. Bring your favorite plushie, wear your most comfy clothes, and join us on the storytime rug for snacks, activities, and the books Not Quite Narwhal and Miss Rita, Mystery Reader.

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

name: The Next Horizon: Navigating the Graduate School Transition
description: This workshop offers a comprehensive overview of the graduate school application process and what to anticipate. We will address key considerations for program selection, understanding admission prerequisites, and developing compelling personal statements. Additionally, you will gain insights into funding opportunities, preparation strategies for standardized examinations, and cultivating strong professional relationships for recommendations. This session aims to equip you with the essential knowledge to confidently pursue your advanced degree aspirations. Register on Handshake: https://unlv.joinhandshake.com/events/1894874/share_preview

name: Denim Day Workshop Series
location: UNLV Student Union & Event Services: ROOM 209
description: Join us for a full day of connection, reflection, and community as we honor Denim Day on April 29. The Care Center is hosting a series of free activities throughout the day to support victim-survivors while promoting education, prevention, and a safer campus. Learn more about the meaning behind Denim Day and take part in spaces designed for healing, creativity, and conversation. Itinerary: 10 a.m. | Sips & Self-Discovery Start your day with a cup of coffee and guided journaling! Explore prompts centered on support, intention-setting, and meaningful moments of connection. In partnership with UNLV Project Wellness. 11 a.m. | Healing in Motion: Yoga Session Take a moment to reset and reconnect through a calming, guided yoga session. Grab free yoga session vouchers! 12 p.m. | Let’s Taco ’Bout Respect: Lunch & Trivia We'll be providing Chipotle during lunch while testing your knowledge on consent, healthy relationships, and bystander intervention. Prizes available for top participants. In partnership with Care Advocates. 1:30 p.m. | Art for Advocacy Engage in creative activities such as bracelet making, bookmark decor, button making, and more. Contribute to collaborative art pieces and learn more about Denim Day and Care Center resources. In partnership with the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art. Stop by at any time or stay the whole day with friends!

name: General Meeting
description: 🏗️ Building Our Future: EWB General Meeting! Are you ready to use your skills to make a global impact? Join Engineers Without Borders for our upcoming general meeting! Whether you're looking to gain hands-on experience, lead a team, or just find a community of like-minded innovators, this is the place to be. 🚀 Major Project Update We’ll be diving into the latest developments on attaining a new project. From technical design 📋 Now Recruiting: Officer Positions We are looking for the next generation of leaders to help drive our mission forward. We have several Officer Positions open and are looking for passionate students to fill them. If you want to play a direct role in shaping the future of our chapter, managing projects, or coordinating outreach, we want to hear from you! Applications and role descriptions will be discussed during the meeting. 🌟 Why Join a Student Organization? Beyond the projects, being part of EWB and the broader student engineering community offers incredible perks: Professional Development: Build your resume with real-world project management and technical design experience. Networking: Connect with university faculty, professional guest speakers, and industry mentors. Leadership Skills: Move beyond the classroom by leading teams and managing budgets. Community: Make lifelong friends who share your passion for civil engineering and global service. 📅 Meeting Details: Date: April 29, 2026 Time: 4:30pm Location: AEB 150 Let’s build something better together. See you there!

name: APIME Mini Con
description: Join us for a half-day experience centered on community-building, history, and challenging dominant narratives through engaging, facilitated conversations. 🗓 Thursday, April 30 ⏰ 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM 📍 Barrick Museum Session 1 | 11:00 AM – 12:15 PM Dreaming Towards a Place for APIME Communities: Between Vision and Action Explore how Asian, Pacific Islander, and Middle Eastern communities have created spaces to thrive through art, activism, and social justice—and imagine what that could look like in Las Vegas. 🍽 Lunch | 12:15 PM – 12:45 PM Take a break, connect with others, and enjoy lunch on us! Session 2 | 12:45 PM – 2:00 PM Not Your Model Minority Unpack the Model Minority Myth, its impact across communities, and how we can challenge it to build more inclusive and honest narratives. ✨ What to Expect: Engaging, thought-provoking conversations Opportunities to reflect and connect A welcoming, community-centered space Drop in anytime—attend one session or stay for both. Lunch will be provided. 👉 RSVP today to secure your spot!

name: Community in Action: Food Pantry Helper
description: Come out and give love at one of our last service events for the Spring semester! On Thursday, April 30th meet us on the third floor of the Student Union in Room 316 at 12pm. After food and conversation, we will head out to The Henderson Equality Center to help stuff food bags and organize new stock deliveries for their pantry! The Henderson Equality Center food pantry aids the community by providing free, weekly, non-judgmental access to frozen, refrigerated, and dry food. Additionally, the center offers free clothing, hygiene produces, school supplies, and even wellness resources. Please come in comfortable clothing and close toes shoes. We will leave campus by 12:30pm, arrive around 1pm and help for two hours. Food will be provided so keep an eye on your email for more details on that!

name: WQP: End of Semester Celebration
description: The Warrior Queen Project: A Women's Empowerment Program follows Swati Bhise's Warrior Queen Action Tool Kit that highlights: Voice, Determination, Sisterhood, Boldness, and Resilience. The program allows for a safe environment where individuals can be open with one another. Students in the program will learn skills for life, listen to guest speakers, make friends, and be a part of an empowering community. We will meet at the Student Union third floor conference room 316 inside the office of Service, Learning, & Leadership at 12:00 PM, we will provide transportation for up to 10 people. name: The Wildlife Society-Herpetofauna Surveys: Chuckwalla Count at Sloan Canyon
location: Meet at the detention basin at the corner of Democracy Drive and Via Firenze in Henderson, NV.
description: We have an exciting field opportunity with the Nevada Department of Wildlife (NDOW) to assist with chuckwalla surveys at Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area. Chuckwallas are large, herbivorous lizards native to the arid rocky deserts of the southwestern U.S. Join NDOW biologists in the field and gain hands-on experience working with native reptiles while contributing to ongoing wildlife monitoring efforts. Spots are limited and filling quickly: Saturday (May 2): 5 spots remaining Sunday (May 3): 5 spots remaining Event Details Dates: Saturday, May 2 & Sunday, May 3, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM Location: Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area This is an excellent opportunity to: Work directly with NDOW biologists Gain field experience in wildlife surveys Build experience for careers in wildlife biology and conservation Sign up here: https://ndow.galaxydigital. com/need/detail/?need_id= 1214059 Due to limited availability, we encourage you to sign up as soon as possible. We hope to see some of you out there!

Attending for an hour will count as either an LSSA club credit or a professional development credit (depending on whichever one needs to be met). Sessions will be online and in-person with online attendance being in our discord (for further information please reach out to us via email at lssa@unlv.edu). Use the link below to fill out our interest! LSSA Finals Study Session Interest Form

name: It’s Hard To Say Goodbye
description: As the semester comes to an end, so do our meetings! Finish your semester out at Perfume Club, where we will have a potluck-style send-off to commemorate our Spring semester! Feel free to bring friends, guests, and even your professor! The only thing we turn our noses at is Black Pepper (seriously, that oil is way too strong) We hope to see you there!!! Please join the GroupMe through the front page link or the QR code on the event flyer to stay up-to-date with club updates and further information.

organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Join us for guided meditation. No registration needed.

name: SORCE Pop Up: Study Breaker
description: Your ultimate finals survival starts here! Refuel with a free lunch and hit the reset button with games, movies, and crafts before you head back to the grind. Join us Thursday, May 7th from 12pm-1:30pm in the SORCE Room (SU 305).

name: Final Study Breakers - Karaoke
description: Take a break from finals, grab the mic, and sing your stress away! Join us for a fun karaoke study break to relax and recharge.

name: Narcan Training
location: Health Education Room 1502, 1st Floor of Student Recreation & Wellness Center
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Join us for a Narcan Training focused on opioid overdose awareness and response. Participants will learn what opioids are, how to recognize the signs of an overdose, and the steps to safely intervene and help save a life if ever needed. This training centers on harm reduction, community care, and building confidence to respond in an emergency. Attendees will receive free Narcan, a CPR mask, and a first aid kit to take home. **Chick-fil-A will be provided while supplies last on a first-come, first-served basis.**

name: Cafe Berlin - End of Semester Dinner
description: Join club members for a fun dinner and socializing at Café Berlin, a local family-owned German restaurant featuring specialties including schnitzel, wurst, yummy desserts and more. It's pay your own way; separate checks are available. Space is limited, so RSVPs are required.

name: Late Night Breakfast
description: Join REB for the last event of the academic year, Late Night Breakfast filled with lots of free food, giveaways, and activities. Come celebrate finals with us!

name: Senior Sendoff
description: One Last Hurrah with the Class of 2026 From the first day of freshman year to the final walk across the stage, we’ve been through it all together. Before you head off on your next great adventure, join us for an evening of reflection, laughter, and celebration. Come enjoy a meal with friends and share those "remember when" stories one last time. Let’s make this final chapter a beautiful one.

name: Feed My Sheep
description: ✨ Feed My Sheep Ministry – Volunteer Opportunity ✨ On the first Sunday of every month, the Newman Center hosts the Feed My Sheep Ministry.
